title: Tandoori Chicken Charcoal Grilled

seo.imageAlt: Close-up of vibrant red Charcoal Grilled Tandoori Chicken skewers on a hot grill, garnished with cilantro.
seo.imageCaption: Experience the smoky, authentic flavors of India with our easy Charcoal Grilled Tandoori Chicken recipe!

description: Experience the iconic flavors of India with this authentic Charcoal Grilled Tandoori Chicken. Marinated in a rich blend of yogurt and aromatic spices, the chicken is cooked to smoky perfection over glowing embers, delivering a tender, juicy, and irresistibly charred delight. This classic dish is a staple for Indian dinners, offering a truly memorable culinary journey.

ingredients.notes: skin removed, cut into 8-10 pieces, deep gashes made
or to taste, for second marinade

instructions.title: Prepare the Chicken and First Marinade
instructions.text: Wash and pat dry the chicken pieces thoroughly. Make deep gashes on the chicken pieces to allow the marinade to penetrate. In a bowl, combine 2 tablespoons of ginger-garlic paste, 2 tablespoons of lemon juice, and 1 teaspoon of salt. Apply this mixture evenly over all chicken pieces, ensuring it gets into the gashes. Let it rest for 15-20 minutes.

instructions.title: Prepare the Second Marinade
instructions.text: In a large mixing bowl, whisk the thick plain yogurt until smooth. Add the remaining 1 tablespoon of ginger-garlic paste, red chili powder, turmeric powder, coriander powder, cumin powder, garam masala, crushed fenugreek leaves, mustard oil, red food color (if using), and 1 teaspoon of salt. Mix all ingredients thoroughly to form a smooth, vibrant marinade.

instructions.title: Marinate the Chicken
instructions.text: Add the chicken pieces from the first marinade to the second marinade, ensuring each piece is fully coated. Cover the bowl and refrigerate for at least 4-6 hours, or preferably overnight, for best flavor and tenderness. The longer the marination, the better the taste.

instructions.title: Prepare the Charcoal Grill
instructions.text: About 30-40 minutes before cooking, prepare your charcoal grill. Arrange the charcoal to create a two-zone fire (direct and indirect heat). Let the coals burn until they are covered with a thin layer of grey ash and are glowing red. This indicates they are ready for grilling.

instructions.title: Grill the Tandoori Chicken
instructions.text: Thread the marinated chicken pieces onto skewers. Place the skewers over the direct heat first for about 5-7 minutes per side, turning frequently to get a nice char. Then, move them to the indirect heat zone to cook through slowly, turning and basting with butter or oil every 5-7 minutes. Continue cooking for a total of 25-30 minutes, or until the chicken is cooked through and the internal temperature reaches 74°C (165°F).

instructions.title: Achieve Smoky Flavor and Serve
instructions.text: For an extra smoky flavor, you can place a small piece of hot charcoal in a small metal bowl directly on the grill, add a teaspoon of ghee or oil, and immediately cover the grill for a few minutes. Remove the chicken from the skewers. Garnish with fresh cilantro and serve hot with lemon wedges, onion rings, and mint chutney.

tips: Always use thick, hung yogurt for the marinade. Draining excess water prevents the marinade from becoming too runny and helps it adhere better to the chicken.
For maximum flavor, marinate the chicken for at least 12-24 hours. The longer the chicken soaks in the spices, the more tender and flavorful it becomes.

To prevent chicken from sticking to the grill, ensure your grill grates are clean and well-oiled before placing the chicken. Basting with butter or oil during grilling also keeps it moist and adds flavor.
article:
Embark on a culinary journey to the vibrant streets and bustling kitchens of India, right from your own backyard! Few dishes capture the essence of Indian grilling quite like Tandoori Chicken. While traditionally cooked in a scorching clay tandoor oven, mastering the art of Charcoal Grilled Tandoori Chicken at home brings an unparalleled depth of smoky flavor and succulent tenderness that simply can't be replicated by other methods. This iconic Indian dinner recipe is a feast for the senses, promising juicy, spice-infused chicken with perfectly charred edges – a true showstopper for any gathering.

## What is Charcoal Grilled Tandoori Chicken?

At its heart, Tandoori Chicken is a classic Indian dish featuring chicken marinated in a rich, spiced yogurt mixture, then traditionally cooked at high heat in a tandoor oven. The yogurt acts as a tenderizer, while a blend of aromatic spices like ginger, garlic, turmeric, cumin, coriander, and garam masala infuses the meat with its characteristic fiery red (or natural orange) hue and complex flavor. When we talk about *Charcoal Grilled Tandoori Chicken*, we're referring to adapting this traditional recipe for a standard backyard charcoal grill. The charcoal imparts a distinctive smoky flavor that beautifully complements the marinade's spices, creating a taste profile that is both authentic and incredibly satisfying. The direct heat of the charcoal grill sears the chicken, locking in juices and creating those coveted charred spots that are crispy and bursting with flavor, making it a truly irresistible Indian dinner recipe.

## Ingredients You Need for Tandoori Chicken

To create this magnificent Charcoal Grilled Tandoori Chicken, you'll need a few key components:

**For the Chicken:**
*   **Chicken:** 2-3 lbs bone-in, skinless chicken pieces (thighs, drumsticks, or a mix are ideal). You can also use a whole spatchcocked chicken.

**For the Marinade:**
*   **Plain Full-Fat Yogurt:** 1 cup (Greek yogurt works well for its thickness).
*   **Ginger-Garlic Paste:** 2 tablespoons (equal parts fresh ginger and garlic, crushed).
*   **Lemon Juice:** 2 tablespoons, freshly squeezed.
*   **Red Chili Powder:** 1-2 teaspoons (adjust to your spice preference; Kashmiri chili powder gives a vibrant red color without excessive heat).
*   **Ground Cumin:** 1 teaspoon.
*   **Ground Coriander:** 1 teaspoon.
*   **Turmeric Powder:** 1/2 teaspoon.
*   **Garam Masala:** 1 teaspoon.
*   **Salt:** 1-1.5 teaspoons, or to taste.
*   **Optional:** A few drops of red food coloring (for the classic vibrant red hue).

**For Grilling & Basting:**
*   **Vegetable Oil or Ghee:** 2 tablespoons, for basting during grilling.
*   **Charcoal:** Hardwood lump charcoal or briquettes.

**Equipment:**
*   Large mixing bowl
*   Sharp knife for scoring
*   Charcoal grill
*   Tongs
*   Basting brush
*   Instant-read meat thermometer

## Step-by-Step Instructions for Grilling Tandoori Chicken

Creating delicious *Charcoal Grilled Tandoori Chicken* is a rewarding process. Follow these steps for perfect results:

1.  **Prepare the Chicken:** Thoroughly wash and pat the chicken pieces dry with paper towels. Using a sharp knife, make 2-3 deep slits on each piece of chicken. This allows the marinade to penetrate deeply and helps the chicken cook evenly.
2.  **Make the Marinade:** In a large mixing bowl, combine the yogurt, ginger-garlic paste, lemon juice, red chili powder, cumin, coriander, turmeric, garam masala, and salt. If using, add a few drops of red food coloring. Mix everything thoroughly until well combined.
3.  **Marinate the Chicken:** Add the scored chicken pieces to the marinade, ensuring each piece is fully coated. Cover the bowl and refrigerate for at least 4 hours, or preferably overnight (up to 24 hours) for maximum flavor and tenderness.
4.  **Prepare Your Charcoal Grill:** About 30-45 minutes before grilling, prepare your charcoal grill. Arrange the coals for a two-zone fire: one side with direct high heat and the other with indirect medium heat. Aim for a grill temperature of around 400-450°F (200-230°C). Clean and oil your grill grates to prevent sticking.
5.  **Grill the Chicken:** Remove the chicken from the marinade, letting any excess drip off. Place the chicken pieces directly over the high-heat side of the grill. Sear for 2-3 minutes per side until you get a nice char.
6.  **Cook Indirectly:** Once seared, move the chicken to the indirect heat side of the grill. Close the lid and continue to cook, turning occasionally, for about 20-30 minutes, or until the internal temperature reaches 165°F (74°C). During the last 10 minutes of cooking, baste the chicken with oil or ghee to keep it moist and enhance browning.
7.  **Rest the Chicken:** Once cooked, remove the *Charcoal Grilled Tandoori Chicken* from the grill and let it rest on a cutting board, loosely tented with foil, for 5-10 minutes. This allows the juices to redistribute, ensuring moist and flavorful chicken.

## Pro Tips for the Best Charcoal Grilled Tandoori Chicken

Achieving restaurant-quality results with your Tandoori Chicken involves a few expert tricks:

*   **Marination Time is Key:** Don't skimp on marination. The longer the chicken marinates (up to 24 hours), the more flavorful and tender it will be. The yogurt works wonders on the chicken's texture.
*   **Score Deeply:** Making deep slits in the chicken allows the marinade to penetrate to the bone, infusing every bite with flavor and helping it cook more evenly.
*   **Use Full-Fat Yogurt:** Full-fat plain yogurt or Greek yogurt provides the best base for the marinade. Its thickness adheres better to the chicken, and its fat content helps keep the chicken moist.
*   **Two-Zone Fire:** This is crucial for *Charcoal Grilled Tandoori Chicken*. Starting with direct heat gives you that beautiful char, while finishing on indirect heat ensures the chicken cooks through without burning the exterior.
*   **Don't Overcrowd the Grill:** Cook in batches if necessary. Overcrowding lowers the grill temperature and steams the chicken instead of grilling it, preventing proper charring.
*   **Baste for Moisture and Flavor:** Basting with oil or ghee during the final stages of grilling adds moisture, promotes browning, and builds another layer of flavor.
*   **Trust Your Thermometer:** An instant-read meat thermometer is your best friend. Chicken is safely cooked when it reaches an internal temperature of 165°F (74°C). This prevents both undercooking and overcooking.
*   **Rest, Rest, Rest:** Resting the chicken after grilling is non-negotiable. It allows the muscle fibers to relax and reabsorb juices, resulting in incredibly moist and tender meat.

## Serving Suggestions for Indian Grilled Chicken

Charcoal Grilled Tandoori Chicken is a star on its own, but it truly shines when paired with traditional Indian accompaniments.

*   **Naan or Roti:** Warm, fluffy naan bread or whole wheat roti are perfect for scooping up the chicken and its delicious juices.
*   **Mint Chutney:** A refreshing mint and coriander chutney provides a cooling contrast to the spiced chicken.
*   **Raita:** A cool, creamy yogurt-based side dish, often with cucumber or onion, also helps balance the heat.
*   **Sliced Onions and Lemon Wedges:** A simple garnish of thinly sliced red onions and a squeeze of fresh lemon juice adds a zesty, sharp counterpoint.
*   **Fresh Salad:** A light kachumber salad (diced cucumber, tomato, onion with a lemon dressing) offers a refreshing crunch.
*   **Basmati Rice:** A side of fragrant basmati rice or jeera rice (cumin rice) makes for a more substantial meal.
*   **As Part of a Mezze:** Serve alongside other Indian appetizers like samosas, pakoras, or paneer tikka for a grand spread.

## Variations and Substitutions for Tandoori Chicken

While the classic *Charcoal Grilled Tandoori Chicken* is hard to beat, there are many ways to customize this recipe to suit your preferences or dietary needs.

*   **Chicken Cuts:** Experiment with boneless chicken thighs for quicker cooking, or even chicken wings for a fantastic appetizer. A whole spatchcocked chicken also works wonderfully on the grill.
*   **Vegetarian Options:** The tandoori marinade is incredibly versatile. Try marinating cubes of paneer (Indian cheese), firm tofu, mushrooms, or cauliflower florets for a delicious vegetarian or vegan alternative. Skewer them for easy grilling.
*   **Spice Level:** Adjust the amount of red chili powder to your liking. For a milder flavor, reduce it; for more heat, add a pinch of cayenne pepper.
*   **Smoked Paprika:** While not traditional, a touch of smoked paprika can enhance the smoky flavor, especially if you're using a gas grill instead of charcoal.
*   **Alternative Cooking Methods:** If a charcoal grill isn't an option, you can still enjoy Tandoori Chicken. A gas grill will give you similar charring, though less smokiness. An oven broiler or even an air fryer can also produce good results, but for that authentic smoky depth, the charcoal grill is truly superior for making Indian dinner recipes like this.

Making Charcoal Grilled Tandoori Chicken at home is an incredibly rewarding experience. The aroma of spices mingling with woodsmoke will tantalize your senses, and the succulent, perfectly charred chicken will undoubtedly become a favorite Indian dinner recipe in your repertoire. So fire up your grill, embrace the flavors, and prepare to impress your family and friends with this truly spectacular dish!
